title: King and Queen Give Special Money in Easter Tradition
summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla went to a special church service in Wales to give away money. This happens every year before Easter and is called the Maundy service. It is a very old tradition that started over 800 years ago. The King and Queen gave gifts to 154 people who help others in their communities. Each person got two purses with special coins. One purse had silver coins that add up to 77 pennies because the King is 77 years old. The other purse had coins to remember the late Queen and a charity. The service was held at St Asaph Cathedral, and it was only the second time it happened in Wales. People at the church were happy and honored to have the King and Queen visit. The service reminds everyone to be humble and serve others, just like Jesus did at the Last Supper.

questions.question: What does the Maundy service remind people to do?
How old is the Maundy service tradition?

background_read: The Maundy service is an ancient Christian tradition that happens on Maundy Thursday, the day before Good Friday. It remembers the Last Supper when Jesus washed his disciples' feet to show humility. Kings and queens in the UK have been doing this for centuries to honor people who help others in their communities. The service includes giving special coins as gifts. This year, King Charles III and Queen Camilla went to Wales for the service, which is rare and special for the people there.
Article_Structure: WHO: King Charles III and Queen Camilla, along with 154 people from communities
WHAT: They attended the Maundy service to give away special coins as gifts
WHERE: At St Asaph Cathedral in north Wales
WHY: To celebrate an ancient Easter tradition that teaches humility and service to others

perspectives.description: People like Susan and Roger Whitehouse who serve their communities without seeking recognition, feeling surprised and honored to be chosen.
